title: Read two-way frequency tables
description: Practice interpreting two-way frequency tables. For example, what does the cell in the top left tell you?
topic_path: Topic C: Lessons 9-10: Bivariate categorical data

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers once surveyed college students on their Facebook use. The following two-way table displays data for the  sample of students who responded to the survey.

**How many students in the survey were in the age category of $18$ to $22?$**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] students

Age | Uses Facebook | Does not use Facebook
- | :-: |:-:
$18$ to $22$ | $78$ | $4$
$23\,\text{+}$ | $70$ | $67$

[//]: # (A. Karpinski and A. Duberstein, “A description of Facebook use and academic performance among undergraduate and graduate students,” paper presented at the American Educational Research Association annual meeting, April 2009. Thanks to Aryn Karpinski for providing us with some original data from the study.)
hints: To find how many students in the survey were in the age category of $18$ to $22,$ we can add to find the total number of students in that row.

Age | Uses Facebook | Does not use Facebook | TOTAL
- | :-: |:-: |:-:
$18$ to $22$ | $\blueD{78}$ | $\blueD{4}$ | $\blueD{82}$
$23\,\text{+}$ | $70$ | $67$ | $137$
**TOTAL** | $148$ | $71$ | $219$
There were $82$ students in the survey in the age category of $18$ to $22$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers once surveyed students on which superpower they would most like to have. The following two-way table displays data for the  sample of students who responded to the survey.

**How many students in the survey chose to fly as their superpower?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] students

Superpower | Male | Female
- | :-: |:-:
Flying | $26$ | $12$
Invisibility | $14$ | $32$
Other | $10$ | $8$
hints: To find how many students in the survey chose to fly as their superpower, we can add to find the total number of students in that row.

Superpower | Male | Female | TOTAL
- | :-: |:-: |:-: |
Flying | $\blueD{26}$ | $\blueD{12}$ | $\blueD{38}$
Invisibility | $14$ | $32$ | $46$
Other | $10$ | $8$ | $18$
**TOTAL** | $50$ | $52$ | $102$
There were $38$ students in the survey who chose to fly as their superpower.

assessment_items.question_content:
A university surveyed its students on their opinions of campus housing. The following two-way table displays data for the sample of $200$ students who responded to the survey.

**How many students in this sample were female?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] students

Gender | Positive opinion | Negative opinion | Neutral opinion
- | :-: |:-: | :-:
Male | $40$ | $36$ | $14$ 
Female | $42$ | $56$ | $12$
hints: To find how many students in this sample were female, we can add to find the total number of students in that row.

Gender | Positive opinion | Negative opinion | Neutral opinion | TOTAL
- | :-: |:-: | :-: | :-:
Male | $40$ | $36$ | $14$ | $90$
Female | $\blueD{42}$ | $\blueD{56}$ | $\blueD{12}$ | $\blueD{110}$
**TOTAL** | $82$ | $92$ | $26$ | $200$
There were $110$ female students in this sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers surveyed recent graduates of two different universities about their income. The following two-way table displays data for the  sample of graduates who responded to the survey.

**How many graduates in the sample had an income of $\$40\text{,}000$ and over?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] graduates

Income | University A | University B
- | :-: |:-: |:-: |
Under $\$20\text{,}000$ | $35$ | $40$ 
$\$20\text{,}000$ to $\$39\text{,}999$ | $90$ | $63$ 
$\$40\text{,}000$ and over| $35$ | $37$
hints: To find how many graduates in the sample had an income of $\$40\text{,}000$ and over, we can add to find the total number of graduates in that row.
There were $72$ graduates in the sample that had an income of  $\$40\text{,}000$ and over.

assessment_items.question_content:
Students were surveyed about their preference between dogs and cats. The following two-way table displays data for the  sample of students who responded to the survey.

**How many students in the sample prefer dogs?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] students  

Preference | Male | Female
- | :-: |:-: |:-: |
Prefers dogs | $36$ | $20$
Prefers cats | $10$ | $26$
No preference | $2$ | $6$
hints: To find how many students in the sample prefer dogs, we can add to find the total number of students in that row.

Preference | Male | Female | TOTAL
- | :-: |:-: |:-: |
Prefers dogs | $\blueD{36}$ | $\blueD{20}$ | $\blueD{56}$
Prefers cats | $10$ | $26$ | $36$
No preference | $2$ | $6$ | $8$
**TOTAL** | $48$ | $52$ | $100$
There were $56$ students in the sample who preferred dogs.

assessment_items.question_content:
Teams in the National Football League in America are divided into two conferences: the AFC and the NFC. Some teams have animals as their mascots, and others do not. The following two-way table displays this data.

**How many teams have an animal mascot?**

[[☃ numeric-input 2]] teams  

Mascot | AFC | NFC 
- | :-: |:-: |:-: |
Animal | $7$ | $8$ 
Non-animal | $9$ | $8$
hints: To find how many teams have an animal mascot, we can add to find the total number of teams in that row.

Mascot | AFC | NFC | TOTAL
- | :-: |:-: |:-: |
Animal | $\blueD7$ | $\blueD8$ | $\blueD{15}$
Non-animal | $9$ | $8$ | $17$
**TOTAL** | $16$ | $16$ | $32$
There are $15$ teams that have an animal mascot.

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers once surveyed college students on their Facebook use. The following two-way table displays data for the  sample of students who responded to the survey.

**How many students in the survey responded that they use Facebook?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] students

$18$ to $22$ | $78$ | $4$
$23\,\text{+}$ | $70$ | $67$

[//]: # (A. Karpinski and A. Duberstein, “A description of Facebook use and academic performance among undergraduate and graduate students,” paper presented at the American Educational Research Association annual meeting, April 2009. Thanks to Aryn Karpinski for providing us with some original data from the study.)

$23\,\text{+}$ | $\blueD{70}$ | $67$ | $137$
**TOTAL** | $\blueD{148}$ | $71$ | $219$

assessment_items.question_content:
A university surveyed its students on their opinions of campus housing. The following two-way table displays data for the sample of $200$ students who responded to the survey.

**How many students in this sample had a negative opinion of campus housing?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] students

Gender | Positive opinion | Negative opinion | Neutral opinion
- | :-: |:-: | :-:
Male | $40$ | $36$ | $14$ 
Female | $42$ | $56$ | $12$
hints: To find how many students in this sample had a negative opinion of campus housing, we can add to find the total number of students in that column.

Gender | Positive opinion | Negative opinion | Neutral opinion | TOTAL
- | :-: |:-: | :-: | :-:
Male | $40$ | $\blueD{36}$ | $14$ | $90$
Female | $42$ | $\blueD{56}$ | $12$ | $110$
**TOTAL** | $82$ | $\blueD{92}$ | $26$ | $200$
There were $92$ students in this sample that had a negative opinion of campus housing.

assessment_items.question_content:
Researchers once surveyed college students on their Facebook use. The following two-way table displays data for the  sample of students who responded to the survey.

**How many $18$ to $22$ year old students in the survey responded that they use Facebook?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] students

Age | Uses Facebook | Does not use Facebook
- | :-: |:-:
$18$ to $22$ | $78$ | $4$
$23\,\text{+}$ | $70$ | $67$

[//]: # (A. Karpinski and A. Duberstein, “A description of Facebook use and academic performance among undergraduate and graduate students,” paper presented at the American Educational Research Association annual meeting, April 2009. Thanks to Aryn Karpinski for providing us with some original data from the study.)
hints: To find how many $18$ to $22$ year old students in the survey responded that they use Facebook, we look for the count in the table that corresponds to both "$18$ to $22$" and "uses Facebook".

Age | Uses Facebook | Does not use Facebook
- | :-: |:-:
$18$ to $22$ | $\blueD{78}$ | $4$
$23\,\text{+}$ | $70$ | $67$
There were $78$ students aged $18$ to $22$ years old  in the survey who responded that they use Facebook

Teams in the National Football League in America are divided into two conferences: the AFC and the NFC. Some teams have animals as their mascots, and others do not. The following two-way table displays this data.
